title: West Chester Celtic Crawl — March 7
summary: West Chester's annual Celtic Crawl bar crawl runs noon to 8 PM on March 7, taking participants through downtown's best pubs.
description:
The West Chester Celtic Crawl is back on Saturday, March 7 from noon to 8 PM. The annual bar crawl takes participants through West Chester's most popular pubs and bars along the downtown corridor. It's one of the borough's biggest social events of the spring season.

If you're heading out for the crawl, it's worth noting that West Chester's sidewalks and older buildings can be uneven — especially after a few stops. Watch your step, and if you've got an older family member at home, this is a good reminder to check that their bathroom is set up safely for when everyone gets back.

title: Trivia Night for Chester County Hospital — March 7
summary: Trivia Night at Hershey's Mill Golf Club on March 7 from 5-8 PM benefits Chester County Hospital's expanded cancer care program.
description:
Hershey's Mill Golf Club hosts a Trivia Night on Friday, March 7 from 5 to 8 PM. The evening includes a taco bar buffet and proceeds support the expanded cancer care program at Chester County Hospital.

Chester County Hospital serves thousands of West Chester residents every year — including the many who end up in the ER after bathroom falls. Supporting local healthcare is one way to help. Making sure your bathroom has grab bars is another.

title: Cheers for Change — Safe Harbor's 30th Anniversary at Teca
summary: Safe Harbor celebrates 30 years on March 11 at Teca on E. Gay Street with a butter dinner and open bar, 6-7:30 PM.
description:
Safe Harbor, a Chester County nonprofit, is celebrating its 30th anniversary on Tuesday, March 11 at Teca (38 E. Gay Street) from 6 to 7:30 PM. The evening features a butter dinner and open bar.

Safe Harbor has spent three decades making Chester County a safer place to live. At Safe Bath, we share that mission — one bathroom at a time. If you or a loved one in West Chester needs grab bars, a shower seat, or any safety modification, we're here.

title: Science on Tap — Kinesiology at Barnaby's, March 17
summary: WCU professor talks kinesiology and movement research at Barnaby's Restaurant on St. Patrick's Day, 6 PM.
description:
On St. Patrick's Day, March 17, West Chester University's Science on Tap series returns to Barnaby's Restaurant at 6 PM. A WCU kinesiology professor will share current research on human movement, balance, and injury prevention.

Kinesiology is the science of how our bodies move — and how that changes as we age. Balance and grip strength are two of the biggest factors in fall prevention for older adults. It's exactly why grab bars exist: to give your body a stable handhold when balance isn't what it used to be.

title: The Doo Wop Project at WCU — March 28
summary: The Doo Wop Project performs 'Echoes of the Street' at West Chester University's Asplundh Concert Hall on March 28 at 7 PM.
description:
The Doo Wop Project brings their 90-minute show "Echoes of the Street" to West Chester University's Emilie K. Asplundh Concert Hall on Saturday, March 28 at 7 PM. The group features stars from Broadway's Jersey Boys and Motown: The Musical.

Asplundh Concert Hall is one of West Chester's best venues. If you're planning a night out, enjoy it — and if you have a parent or grandparent who might want to come along, make sure they can get ready safely at home. A grab bar in the shower or beside the toilet costs $199 and installs in under an hour.
